subject: Biology
title: The Open Ocean: A Blue Desert
description: A quick, practical guide to The Open Ocean: A Blue Desert.

hook.text: The open ocean covers more area than all land combined—but it's mostly a biological desert.

buildup.text: Nutrients sink and sunlight only reaches the top 200 meters, limiting where life can thrive.

discovery.text: Upwelling zones where cold, nutrient-rich water rises create hotspots of incredible productivity.

twist.text: Tuna, swordfish, and mahi-mahi migrate thousands of kilometers following these productive patches.

climax.text: The pelagic zone may look empty, but its total biomass outweighs all life on land.

punchline.text: Looks empty. Contains more life than every continent combined.

quiz.question: What creates productive hotspots in the open ocean?
options: Upwelling zones bringing nutrient-rich water to the surface
